page_title: The Swashbuckling Adventures of Dino Hunter
text:
The Swashbuckling Adventures of Dino Hunter (ディノハンター, Bōken Katsugeki Dino Hantā) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 395 is an action-adventure book that features its eponymous protagonist character going on many adventures and journeying to many unknown places. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 0, Chapter 1 

Background

Chronologically, the book is first seen in Meteor City with a young Sheila, who aspires to become a Hunter one day. Years later, Sheila collapses somewhere in the Lukso Province, where she is found and rescued by Kurapika and Pairo. As thanks for helping her, she gives them her copy of the book. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

In a flashback showing the formation of the Phantom Troupe, Pakunoda asks Sheila and Sarasa if they want to help with a dubbing project. After the two agree, Pakunoda asks Sheila what book she’s holding, and Sheila says it’s The Swashbuckling Adventures of Dino Hunter. Pakunoda then wonders if she has already read it, and Sheila replies that it’s the only book she reads because she is going to become a Hunter one day. Pakunoda points out that she should read a lot of books, but Sheila tells her to leave her alone and that she will make it work. 

Kurapika's Memories

In a flashback within a flashback, Kurapika and Pairo find Sheila lying with an injured leg in the forest as she desperately asks for water. With the two unable to understand her, Pairo says that she's speaking the language of the outside world. Sheila points to her canteen, and they realize what she wants, with Kurapika quickly running to a nearby river. After drinking, Sheila thanks them and gives them Dino Hunter as a gift. The two are left unsure, and Pairo worries that people will know they spoke to an outsider if they take it. Sheila tries to ask them about the river so she can find more water, but the language barrier continues to be an issue. Kurapika then heads back to the village to get his father's dictionary, and Pairo worries that he will be punished if they find out what they're doing. 

Kurapika explains that the book Sheila gave them was an action-adventure story about someone who worked as a Hunter, inspiring Sheila herself to become a professional Hunter. He adds that he and Pairo kept the book in a secret location and would read it every day using his father's dictionary. The two then discover a letter from Sheila thanking them for their help and hoping to meet them in the outside world, adding that she hopes to be a professional Hunter by then. Pairo guesses that it would have been too hard for her to say goodbye in person, and Kurapika narrates that they were able to read the letter without the dictionary at that point, adding that they continued to read the book every day and that it made him even more eager to visit the outside world. 

Back in the main flashback, Kurapika reaches the Elder and angrily asks him what he did with the book. He says that he doesn't know, but Kurapika tells him to give it back since it means a lot to him. The Elder says that he can have it when he grows up since it has given him an idealized fantasy about the outside world, emphasizing that not all humans are so nice. Kurapika is surprised that he read it and eagerly asks what he thinks of it. After being taken aback by Kurapika's enthusiasm, the Elder calls it a fine book filled with courage and dreams, adding that it even excited an old man like himself. However, he stresses that it's still just a fantasy, and Kurapika says that the fantasy gave him the dream to go to the outside world, wondering if it's wrong to have such a dream. As the Elder thinks about various scenes from the book, he begins to talk about the problem being reality, but Kurapika interrupts him, angrily asking what part of Dino Hunter he read. He then stresses that the book is trying to teach them to overcome those obstacles and live the best they can, asking what the point of reality is if it loses to a book. 

After journeying to the outside world, Kurapika says that they were able to finish their shopping so quickly because of everyone's help. He reminds Pairo that Dino Hunter said there will always be people willing to help no matter what. Later, as Kurapika prepares to leave for the outside world, Pairo tells him to take care of himself, and Kurapika promises to find a doctor who can heal him, asking him to pass the exam too so that they can go on a trip together. Pairo promises to pass the exam and adds that while he's happy Kurapika wants to find a doctor for him, the reason they want to see the outside world is because they wanted an enjoyable adventure like the one in Dino Hunter. He then tells Kurapika that when he returns, he will ask him if he had fun. He asks him to promise to have a trip where he can answer "yes" from the bottom of his heart. Kurapika happily promises and then rides off as Pairo and the other members wave goodbye. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 0, Chapter 2
